body:
Peter,

The **Democrats are in complete disarray.**

This week, Hillary Clinton, Barack Obama, and the liberal Hollywood elite are celebrating Hillary's coronation as the Democratic Party's nominee.

Meanwhile, millions of grassroots Democrats are outraged over the DNC's emails that were released that proved the Democratic Establishment's bias against Bernie Sanders. _**The Democratic Party is bitterly divided as it enters the general election, never a good place to be.**_

Despite their terrible candidate and the American people's desire for change after eight years of Obama, the Democrats still had wall-to-wall media fawning coverage at their convention this week from the liberal mainstream media. Republicans are counting on you to help us counteract this. **If we do not take action now, the Democrats could secure a MAJOR advantage in fundraising and the polls over our Republican campaigns. **
**We only have 1 day left until our July finance deadline. **

subject: the future of our party

body:
Peter,

The future of the Republican Party - and frankly, the future of
this country - is on the line this year.

The Democrats will do anything they can to exploit an
opportunity, and that's what they're doing right now.

Republicans have 24 Senate seats up this election cycle. Many of
our Senate races are in battleground states, including my home
state of Ohio. The Democrats know this, and they have become
obsessed with turning the Senate blue to advance their extreme
liberal agenda.

Our only chance to stop them is to take action right now.

Contribute $25, $50, $100 or any amount you can give to help
Republicans maintain our Senate majority this
year.https://contribute.nrsc.org/quadruple-match/?recurring=true&fname=Peter&lname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cs-kfp&utm_campaign=20160730_John-Kasich_cs-kfp&utm_content=body_txt_contribute2550100&action=email_click&ha1=

| Greetings,
---
| We are less than two weeks away from the August 9 Primary election day and early voting is still underway. So before we dive into this week’s actions and events, we want to first **thank you for all of your hard work** through the Primary. We must keep this momentum going to **ensure Phil becomes the Republican nominee for Governor** and we send a strong message for the General Election! Whether you vote early or get to the polls on August 9, **_make sure you vote for Phil_ and encourage your friends, family and neighbors to vote too!** Let’s keep going!

Now, back to our regularly scheduled programming. A few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign are below.

**TWO BIG ENDORSEMENTS: MANCHESTER JOURNAL & VEHICLE AND AUTOMOTIVE DISTRIBUTORS ASSOCIATION**
Building on his strong support from thousands of Vermonters and the endorsement of 54 Republican legislators, **Phil earned the endorsement of the Vermont Vehicle and Automotive Distributors Association (VADA), and of the Manchester Journal**.

**_VADA_** cited Phil’s experience and his proven ability to lead as we work to **build a stronger economy,_urging Vermonters to vote for Phil in the August 9 primary_**. Joining Phil at **Goss Dodge Chrysler in South Burlington,** VADA highlighted **Phil’s commitment to addressing our crisis of affordability and restore faith and trust in government.** Read the official press release [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1f192e3e79&e=19cebfe173).

**_The Manchester Journal_** also voiced its support for Phil, endorsing him in the Republican Primary. “**We like Mr. Scott for his _focus on the economy and concern for its ‘affordability’_ for the average Vermonter**. Vermont’s got a lot going for it, but one thing it doesn’t have at present is an economy stuffed with good-paying jobs that makes living here an easily affordable project,” the Journal said. The paper goes on to endorse a number of Phil’s proposals. Read the official press release [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d7a0a8297b&e=19cebfe173) and the full endorsement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1793da967d&e=19cebfe173).
Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT

*******
**PHIL RELEASES HIS TRANSPORTATION PLAN**
On the heels of his VADA endorsement, Phil introduced his transportation priorities, outlining a plan for **improving Vermont’s roads and bridges** , and **reiterating his _opposition to higher transportation taxes, a carbon tax and infrastructure debt_ supported by his opponents**.

Transportation issues impact our entire economy, and Phil has worked to make this sector more efficient throughout his career. **One of his first acts in state government was**[**his work to reduce the Legislature’s raid on the Transportation Fund**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e460c57aa0&e=19cebfe173)**.**

**Highlights from Phil’s plan include** :
* Strengthening the link between economic growth and infrastructure with a comprehensive plan that **streamlines processes and prioritizes maintenance** to reduce costs;
* **_Opposing_****new or higher transportation taxes** ;
* **Ensuring the transportation fund is _not raided_** and is managed properly; and
* **Encouraging innovation** in transportation and other sectors by **restoring the full research and development tax credit**.

To read Phil Scott’s transportation plan click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=acdbbd84e3&e=19cebfe173). You can also click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f3b4702971&e=19cebfe173) to read more about Phil’s plan and his VADA endorsement at VT Digger.
*******
**CLEAR PRIORITIES: PHIL CONTINUES TO BRING HIS POSITIVE MESSAGE TO VERMONTERS**
We hope you saw our new TV ad on the airwaves this week, highlighting Phil’s priorities for Vermont. We want to make certain Phil’s approach to governing is clear, and that ALL Vermonters understand how he will lead Vermont forward! Check the new ad, “Clear Priorities,” out [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0dfed27bba&e=19cebfe173). | [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4dtoQRkpEu4)
---

***

**CONNECTING WITH VERMONT**
We zig-zagged through Vermont this week, working to meet with as many Vermonters as we can to share Phil’s positive message before the August 9 Primary. Phil had a chance to visit the **Chaput family farm in North Troy** , and **the Cow Palace in Derby**. He met with Vermonters in **Hyde Park** and **Joe’s Pond** to learn about their economic challenges and addressed critical issues at forums in **Burlington** and **Grafton**.

Photo Credit: @PhilScott4VT

View our Events page [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3577d9f7b0&e=19cebfe173) for all upcoming events, and see us this weekend in [Lake Bomoseen](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1453476059&e=19cebfe173) and in Barre at the [Rotary Breakfast](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=01455fa5ca&e=19cebfe173), [Truck Pull](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=a79bac3093&e=19cebfe173), at [Maria’s Bagels](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9a2e8ddef0&e=19cebfe173) and in the [Barre Homecoming Parade](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fa40abd669&e=19cebfe173).

***

**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Rep. Tom Burditt: Backing Scott for Governor_**
“We need someone that can do more than just “do” the job. We need Phil Scott. Join me in making Vermont a better place for hard-working Vermonters. Vote Phil Scott for governor in the Republican primary.” – Rep. Tom Burditt (Source: Rutland Herald: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=05d53f43fd&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Honoring Kermit Smith_**
**“** As politicians and public servants we’re trained to weather all kinds of storms, so I think a bridge is a very appropriate monument for a Vermonter who built their reputation on rising above the fray and connecting their community with the rest of the state. Kermit Smith reminds us that making an impact starts at home, in our own towns, and finding ways to reach those on the other side.” (Source: VT Digger: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e4aa659b6c&e=19cebfe173))

**_Voting for Phil Scott_**
“Phil Scott listens to Vermonters. He learns about our problems and wants to make Vermont more affordable for us all. He leads by example: He was on the ground during Tropical Storm Irene – literally moving dirt, helping to get emergency services to the people who needed it most and was the only advocate for folks whose mobile homes were swept away.” _Colette Dublois, Manchester_(Source: Manchester Journal: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=36fcce55e0&e=19cebfe173))

***

**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_Heidi Scheuermann in Stowe Reporter_**
Phil’s grateful for each and every endorsement he receives – whether a formal endorsement from an organization or colleague, or the more informal support he receives from Vermonters through Letters to the Editor, on his social media pages, or as he meets with you around the state. **Rep. Heidi Scheurmann** provided a very strong and thoughtful endorsement of Phil in her article in the **Stowe Reporter** this week, that we recommend you read [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d63b3bff34&e=19cebfe173). She writes passionately about **Phil’s steady hand, his commitment to Vermonters and to his convictions, and his ability to work across the political aisle to move the best solutions forward**.

But, she also outlines **_what is at stake in this election_** , noting “At this time, **_all three of the Democratic candidates_ for governor are proposing _more and higher taxes and fees_** to pay for large, new, expensive government initiatives.” She urges Vermonters to **vote for Phil on Aug. 9 because he is the _best candidate to win in November_ and the _best candidate to run our state_**. Read her full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=865b055dd9&e=19cebfe173).

*******

**Phil and Team Scott are working very hard to share Phil’s positive messages across the state. If you like what our campaign stands for,**[please consider making a donation today!](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4b423311f4&e=19cebfe173)**As we get down to the wire _we cannot take anything for granted_. The punditry may give you a false sense of confidence going into the Primary, but the truth is elections can be unpredictable and _we need to do everything we can in these final weeks to make absolutely certain we get out the vote, and share our message_ with all Vermonters so they _vote for Phil on August 9_. Please help Phil and the team continue to run a positive campaign, one that our children can be proud of! **

[DONATE TODAY!](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8927690b2c&e=19cebfe173)

*******

Thanks to everyone that came out to events and participated in meetings this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=49ef2f06a1&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=96c0154fd0&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott

disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF COLE
body:
| |
---
| Friend --
Jim Justice has pulled out all the stops to keep voters from knowing that he [supports](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3D_hEsnoxhbPY&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=now_we_know&n=1) Hillary Clinton for President. Now we know why. In her acceptance speech at the Democratic National Convention in Philadelphia, Hillary Clinton made it clear she backs an extreme liberal agenda that is out of step with West Virginia.

Hillary Clinton at the very beginning of her [speech](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.latimes.com%2Fpolitics%2Fla-na-pol-hillary-clinton-convention-speech-transcript-20160728-snap-htmlstory.html&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=now_we_know&n=2) pledged to fully implement the Democratic platform calling it the only way to “...turn our progressive platform into real change for America. We wrote it together – now let’s go out there and make it happen together.” The [platform](http://www.billcolewv.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.demconvention.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2016%2F07%2FDemocratic-Party-Platform-7.21.16-no-lines.pdf&e=6827555c47a2f041f636b0c6a0edbd27&utm_source=billcole&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=now_we_know&n=3) calls for an end to fossil fuel production in America, starting with requiring half of all energy production to be from renewable sources within a decade. It calls for an end to mining and a ban on fracking. West Virginia can’t afford four more years of President Obama’s War on Coal, and now Hillary Clinton has not only doubled down on his war, she has pledged to open up a new front on the Washington liberal agenda to end fossil fuel production in America. West Virginia’s history is rooted in producing cheap power for America, and we can’t afford for Hillary Clinton to take more of our good paying jobs.

Jim Justice has called for party unity, and he is running on a national platform that will end energy production in America and a state platform – yet to be published – that will end mining and the ability to harness our vast resources of natural gas that will keep and create thousands of new good paying jobs in West Virginia.

Ensuring that West Virginia continues to be an energy producing state is the main reason I endorsed Donald Trump for President. He will work with me and all West Virginians to get our miners back to work and help us create the good paying jobs we need. name: Michael Tyler, RussFeingold.com
subject: Protect and expand, not slash and gut
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
51 years after they were signed into law, Medicaid and Medicare are on the line. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema") Friend, On this day 51 years ago, President Lyndon B. Johnson signed Medicare and Medicaid into law. President Lyndon B. Johnson's vision was simple: To lend a hand to Americans struggling to get the care they need, and to ensure health care security for retired Americans. These programs have become essential, cherished institutions. Medicaid has provided a safety net for millions of low-income Americans, children, women, and people with disabilities, while Medicare has become a bedrock of American retirement. But Republicans in the party of Trump, like Senator Ron Johnson, have one thing on their minds: slashing and gutting Medicaid and Medicare -- which would be devastating. Senator Johnson even said he'd like to turn Medicare into a voucher program. _It's shameful._ **[On the 51st anniversary of Medicaid and Medicare, we need to commit to expanding and protecting -- not slashing and cutting -- these vital programs. If you agree, add your name next to mine right now.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema")** As Russ has traveled across Wisconsin, he's heard from people of all backgrounds, many of who rely on Medicare and Medicaid. Russ knows these programs are a true lifeline for so many -- not just another program for huge corporate interests and insurance companies to put on the chopping block. It's abundantly clear that Senator Johnson hasn't listened -- _at all_ \-- to the concerns of Wisconsinites whose mothers and fathers, daughters and sons, and sisters and brothers depend on these crucial programs to preserve their health and extend their lives. Billionaires and multi-millionaires like Senator Johnson don't know what it's like to worry about health coverage: they can pay to visit the world's best doctors with cash. Middle-class and working families in Wisconsin can't do that. That's why today, on the 51st anniversary of the creation of Medicare and Medicaid, we must stand together in their defense -- and against politicians like Senator Johnson, who aren't listening to everyday Americans. **[Help us send Senator Johnson a message: We must protect and expand Medicare and Medicaid. Add your name and show you support America's safety net today.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema")** Thanks for taking action. Michael Tyler
Communications Director | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160730-51med?utm_source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&source=ema_2016-07-30_51med&utm_campaign=51med&utm_medium=ema")

clean_body: 51 years after they were signed into law, Medicaid and Medicare are on the line. | "" Friend, On this day 51 years ago, President Lyndon B. Johnson signed Medicare and Medicaid into law. President Lyndon B. Johnson's vision was simple: To lend a hand to Americans struggling to get the care they need, and to ensure health care security for retired Americans. These programs have become essential, cherished institutions. Medicaid has provided a safety net for millions of low-income Americans, children, women, and people with disabilities, while Medicare has become a bedrock of American retirement. But Republicans in the party of Trump, like Senator Ron Johnson, have one thing on their minds: slashing and gutting Medicaid and Medicare -- which would be devastating. Senator Johnson even said he'd like to turn Medicare into a voucher program. It's shameful. "" As Russ has traveled across Wisconsin, he's heard from people of all backgrounds, many of who rely on Medicare and Medicaid. Russ knows these programs are a true lifeline for so many -- not just another program for huge corporate interests and insurance companies to put on the chopping block. It's abundantly clear that Senator Johnson hasn't listened -- at all \-- to the concerns of Wisconsinites whose mothers and fathers, daughters and sons, and sisters and brothers depend on these crucial programs to preserve their health and extend their lives. Billionaires and multi-millionaires like Senator Johnson don't know what it's like to worry about health coverage: they can pay to visit the world's best doctors with cash. Middle-class and working families in Wisconsin can't do that. That's why today, on the 51st anniversary of the creation of Medicare and Medicaid, we must stand together in their defense -- and against politicians like Senator Johnson, who aren't listening to everyday Americans. "" Thanks for taking action. Michael Tyler Communications Director | ""

body:
About those balloons | Peter --

I’m so proud of the convention we put on, and the work our amazing team put into it. But while they were busy arranging that balloon drop (more than 100,000 of them!), I’m crunching a different set of numbers, with my eyes on our larger goal: meeting our July 31st fundraising deadline. And Peter, we’re not close.

We're at a critical point in the race. Hillary and Trump are now officially squaring off against each another, one on one. Every dollar counts. **[I’m looking for 88 people from your area to step up today and make sure we can fund the campaign we’ll need to take on Trump. Will you be one of them, Peter?**](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjYxMTY5ODES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0NjIxNDE5S0&mt=1&rt=0)

After the chaos of the last few weeks (or honestly, months!) I want nothing more than to put my head down and rest. But I can’t. None of us can. **It’s never been as important as it is now to stand with Hillary and show you’re with her. (And when you do, we’ll send you a free sticker, too!) Work now, celebrate later.** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYxMTY5ODES1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0NjIxNDE5S0&mt=1&rt=0)

Dennis

name: Chelsea Clinton
subject: You’ve brought her this far, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Thank you. | Peter --

A long time ago, my mom became famous for saying that it takes a village to raise a child. I hope it was clear Thursday night why I am so proud to be her daughter, and so grateful she is Charlotte and Aidan’s grandmother. I know from the bottom of my heart she will make us all proud as our president.

It turns out it takes a village to elect a president, too. And you’re a part of it.

I’m so grateful to everyone who helped my mom become the Democratic nominee this week -- the volunteers who made phone calls, knocked on doors, and all who donated.

Thank you to everyone who wakes up in the morning determined to make somebody else’s life better, and for working with my mom because she shares that same drive.

Thank you.

**You’ve brought her this far. Can you chip in $1 to help her take the next steps to the White House, where she can continue to fight for us all?** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYxMTcwODQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0NjI3ODk1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: We have a deadline looming, and here’s the poll we’re looking at
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
This race is actually close | Peter --

Let me be straight with you: We have no idea what kind of polling bounce we might get from our convention that just wrapped up. As momentous and energizing as it all was, in the background a national poll came out two days ago that put our lead over Donald Trump at just ONE point.

That’s not just within the margin of error. This race is so close that it’s conceivable we could commit an unforgivable error as a country -- and actually elect Trump.

**[I’m not taking anything for granted, Peter. If you’re just as ready to fight for Hillary as I am, chip in right now, and we’ll send you a free sticker as a thank you.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjYxMTczOTkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0NjM3ODE0S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

We have a huge, end-of-month FEC deadline coming up tomorrow. We need to show the Trump campaign and voters all across America that this team is stronger, smarter, and more ready to win this election. The conventions are over -- now it’s about getting to work, from the ground up.

Our fundraising results put organizers to work and reserve ad time in key battleground states like Ohio and Florida. But they also tell a story about how good our campaign feels. And right now, with the general election beginning in earnest and everyone launching into a sprint, we have to send the loudest, proudest message:

We are the party of inclusion, serious ideas, love, and compassion. **Hillary is going to fight for us now and every day she is in the White House. And our team is going to get her there, starting with your contribution before our MIDNIGHT deadline tomorrow:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYxMTczOTkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0NjM3ODE0S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks -- and let’s go win this election for Hillary,

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America

|
**Hillary needs you now more than ever -- chip in $1 before Sunday's midnight deadline to stand with her:**

| [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYxMTczOTkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQ0NjM3ODE0S0&mt=1&rt=0)

---

**** | | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1.5 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House).
---

name: Evan Bayh
subject: off the sidelines
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, PO Box 133 W
body:
| [ ](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50425/7be36b49/1ed2de25/78f8c18e/3685748009/VEsH/ "http://www.evanbayhforindiana.com/")
---
Friend,

I'm in this race because I believe our government needs a change, and I can no longer stay off the sidelines.

Washington could learn a thing or two from Indiana, where we work together to get things done. That's how it was when I was governor: Democrats and Republicans came together to cut taxes, help Indiana families afford college, support our veterans, and work with business leaders to create jobs. We shared common values and we wanted to find solutions to common problems.

**That 's the future I'll work for as a U.S. Senator.** But I'm going to need your help. **[Will you pitch in whatever you can to my Senate campaign?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50425/7be36b49/1ed2de25/78f8c18d/3685748009/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-julyeom?refcode=20160730&amount=25")** Our first end-of-month deadline hits tomorrow.

We all know what needs to be done: **Politicians need to put country over party.** My record shows that I'm not the type to engage in partisan bickering. I'm a problem solver and a finder of the middle ground. And I'll never put special interests above the right decision for Hoosiers.

**[Friend, if that sounds like what you're looking for in a senator for Indiana, I hope you'll contribute to my campaign before midnight tomorrow.](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50425/7be36b49/1ed2de25/78f8c18d/3685748009/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-julyeom?refcode=20160730&amount=25")**

I appreciate all your help and am looking forward to keeping you updated from the campaign trail.

Evan [Contribute](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50425/7be36b49/1ed2de25/78f8c18d/3685748009/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-julyeom?refcode=20160730&amount=25")
